text: The advent of artificial intelligence (AI) has become more noticeable in the journalistic sector, especially in developed media outlets. Journalists have come to understand the significant impact of AI, indicating that it will remain a helpful assistant to human journalists working inside newsrooms. As AI has become deeply rooted in journalism, many benefits have been achieved, while a number of challenges are still hindering its spread among journalists. Journalists are concerned about ethical issues resulting from the AI technology used in the journalism sector. Therefore, this study investigates the ethical challenges that AI poses to journalists within the framework of technological development and media ethics, which is based on the Social Responsibility Theory as the moral philosophy that supports this study. A qualitative approach was adopted in this study. Interviews were conducted with a number of journalists at Al Mamlaka TV in Jordan, a government-affiliated channel. Through a thematic analysis of the data obtained from the interviews, it was found that the ethical challenges that journalists may face when working in the newsroom with AI are biased data, privacy infringement, and the lack of legislation and international policies that regulate the use of AI in journalism. It was also found that the studied journalists at Al Mamlaka TV are committed to the principles of the Social Responsibility Theory, and work on a critically and constructively adopting AI in the newsroom.
